slots.rod: RIVERTEX Fenix 11'5" (Match Rod, 6.5-17.5 Lb, Mod Fast, Level 23)
slots.terminal_tackle: Sport Bobber (Balsa, X Low sensitivity), Kirby Hook #8

leader_recommendation.why: Short leader for panfish targeting. The fluoro mainline already provides good stealth, but a thinner leader at the terminal end further reduces visibility for line-shy Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish in Falcon Lake's crystal-clear mountain water.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-5 ft for panfish. Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish hold near weed edges and shallow structure. At A Bridge of Logs, try 3-4 ft near the log pilings.

issues: Very minor: #8 hook may miss some of the smallest Redbreast Sunfish — #10 would catch everything but reduces Pumpkinseed hook-up rate
Sport Bobber is not a glowing float — won't be visible if you unlock night fishing later
improvements: MASSIVE upgrade from old setup — purpose-built panfish rig with properly matched components
Fenix 11'5" at level 23 is the best match rod available — Mod-Fast action detects light panfish bites
Sport Bobber's balsa construction with X Low float weight gives maximum sensitivity for tiny bites
Fluoro .009" mainline is nearly invisible in clear water — huge improvement over the old braid
Kirby #8 hook is perfectly sized for Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish
Red Worms cover BOTH mission panfish species — Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish
Can also incidentally catch trout with Red Worms — bonus income while mission fishing
Deploy on TripleFeeder Rod Stand at A Bridge of Logs for passive mission catches

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast)
issues: Nano Spoon can incidentally catch Pumpkinseed but isn't optimized for mission panfish
This setup is better suited as a trout hunter for income between mission fish
improvements: Excellent trout setup — keep this for income fishing between mission catches
Nano Spoon 1/6 Oz covers Rainbow, Cutthroat, and Oregon Redband Trout — all high-value fish at Falcon Lake
Oregon Redband Trout ($200/kg) is the top earner here — this spoon targets them
Light rod + fluoro line is perfectly matched for Falcon Lake

slots.rod: MagFin JigWinner 8'10" (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast, Level 20)
slots.terminal_tackle: Common Jig Head 1/2 Oz. #3/0 with Worm Acid Green 3"
issues: Very minor: 12 Lb fluoro is slightly heavy for lighter trout but ideal for Smallmouth Bass drag fights
Note: JigWinner 8'10" is same model as slot 2 — if you don't want duplicates, the Espira ML 3500 (level 22, $8,800) is a budget reel alternative
improvements: MASSIVE upgrade — purpose-built Smallmouth Bass jig rig replacing the overpowered Loki Heavy setup
Light/Fast rod gives excellent jig sensitivity — you'll feel every bump and tap from bass
12 Lb fluorocarbon is invisible in clear water and strong enough for 4.6 lb Smallmouth with drag headroom
#3/0 jig head matches wiki's recommended hook size for Smallmouth Bass
Worm 3" soft plastic is specifically listed as effective for Smallmouth Bass at Falcon Lake
1/2 Oz jighead falls perfectly in the rod's 1/6-5/8 Oz range — good bottom contact without being too heavy
Work the jig near rocky structure at Nature's Charming Hardcore — hop it along the bottom with pauses
Alternative: carry Tube baits (Watermelon Seed 2", level 10, $100) as backup — Tubes are also effective on Smallmouth

leader_recommendation.why: Versatile leader for mixed panfish and trout. Crickets attract Pumpkinseed via the insect bait group and are effective on trout too. The medium leader length works for both shallow panfish and mid-depth trout. Falcon Lake's mountain water is crystal clear — stealth matters.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-7 ft. Start at 4 ft for panfish near shore, increase to 6-7 ft when targeting trout in deeper water. At A Bridge of Logs, try 4-5 ft near the log structure.

issues: Durability low (16385/18000) — Falcon Lake trips will wear it further
Hook #6 is good for Pumpkinseed but slightly large for Redbreast Sunfish — #8 would be more versatile
improvements: This is your BEST MISSION SLOT — Crickets + Hook #6 covers Pumpkinseed and trout
Consider downsizing to #8 hook to also catch Redbreast Sunfish more effectively
Add a fluorocarbon leader (2-4 Lb, 10-14 in) for clear mountain water
Deploy on TripleFeeder Rod Stand at A Bridge of Logs for passive mission catches
Crickets cover the most species at Falcon Lake of any single bait in your loadout

slots.rod: Aurora 8'2" Sport (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-1/2 Oz, 3-10 Lb, Fast)
issues: Narrow Spoons are excellent for trout but less effective for mission panfish
1/4 Oz is good weight for Falcon Lake depth
improvements: Keep this as your active trout/bass spinning rod — great for income between mission catches
Narrow Spoons are specifically recommended for Oregon Redband Trout ($200/kg)
Also covers Brown Trout which are the heaviest fish at Falcon Lake (8.6 lb max)
Try slow retrieves near Waterfall Cascade and Trout Lair for best trout action

slots.rod: ValueSpin 6'3" (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-6.5 Lb, Fast)
issues: Nano Spinner catches trout and can incidentally catch Pumpkinseed via Casting Spoon category
Mono .010" is slightly visible in Falcon Lake's very clear water
#1/0 hook is large for panfish — will mostly catch trout-sized fish
improvements: Good versatile setup but consider swapping to a Nano Spoon to better target Rock Bass
Rock Bass responds to Nano Spoons per the wiki — this could be a MISSION ROCK BASS slot
Upgrade mono to fluoro for better stealth in crystal-clear mountain water
The short 6'3" rod is ideal for fishing tight spots at A Bridge of Logs

slots.rod: MCT McCarp 13'2" (Match Rod, 9-25.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Longer leader for dedicated trout targeting at Falcon Lake. Trout here run larger than Rocky Lake (Brown Trout up to 8.6 lb, Rainbow up to 8.2 lb) so the heavier 4-8 Lb test is warranted. The longer leader keeps Flies bait well away from the float for natural presentation in the clear mountain water.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 7-13 ft. Falcon Lake is deeper than Rocky Lake. At Trout Lair, start at 10 ft. At Waterfall Cascade, try 8-10 ft near the oxygenated water.

issues: Hook #1/0 is too large for mission panfish but acceptable for trout
Flies don't attract Pumpkinseed, Redbreast Sunfish, or Rock Bass — no mission coverage
13'2" rod is very long for Falcon Lake's tighter fishing spots
improvements: Keep this as a dedicated trout income rig — Flies + float is excellent for Oregon Redband ($200/kg)
Downsize hook to #2 or #4 for better trout bite rate
Add a fluorocarbon leader (4-8 Lb, 14-20 in) for deep trout presentation
Deploy on TripleFeeder Rod Stand at Trout Lair for passive trout income while actively fishing for mission species

passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Log structure attracts panfish (Pumpkinseed, Redbreast Sunfish) and trout cruise nearby. The bridge provides cover that concentrates fish — ideal for passive float rods. Shallow weedy areas along the logs are prime panfish habitat.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast spinning rods from the bridge itself or along the shore within sight of your rod stand. Target trout in deeper water off the logs with slots 2 and 5, or move slightly toward Nature's Charming Hardcore for Smallmouth Bass with slot 3 (but stay close enough to see your floats).
strategy: Set RodPod Trio at A Bridge of Logs — deploy 3 float rods along the log structure
Slot 1 (Fenix + Sport Bobber + Red Worms + #8): Cast near log pilings at 3-4 ft for Pumpkinseed/Redbreast Sunfish (MISSION FISH)
Slot 4 (OmniFloat + Crickets + #6): Cast to mid-depth (4-6 ft) near weed edges for panfish + incidental trout
Slot 7 (McCarp + Flies + #1/0): Cast to deeper water (8-10 ft) for trout — Oregon Redband Trout ($200/kg) income
Actively fish slot 2 (Nano Spoon) or slot 5 (Narrow Spoon) casting from the bridge for trout
Watch the Sport Bobber on slot 1 closely — its X Low sensitivity shows very subtle panfish bites as tiny dips
Panfish bite quickly — switch to the rod within 3-5 seconds of the float moving
Trout bites on slot 7 are more deliberate — the glowing float will pull under steadily
MISSION TIP: The 3 passive float rods will catch Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish while you actively hunt trout for income — dual purpose
income_estimate: Passive stand rods: Slots 1 & 4 catch panfish ($70-170/kg, small fish) + slot 7 catches trout ($170-200/kg)
Active spinning: Slots 2 & 5 target Oregon Redband ($200/kg, up to 4 lb) and Rainbow Trout ($170/kg, up to 8.2 lb)
Mission fish from passive rods: Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish accumulate while you focus on trout income
Estimated passive income: $300-$600/hr from stand rods (mix of panfish + trout)
Estimated active income: $600-$1,200/hr from spinning trout
Combined: $900-$1,800 per in-game hour. Plus mission completion XP bonuses.

notes: FishCastle L — fill with high-value trout (Oregon Redband, Rainbow, Brown) between mission catches
TripleFeeder Rod Stand — deploy float rods (slots 1, 4, 7) for passive mission catches while actively spinning
Travel cost to Falcon Lake is $4,000 and extend is $900/day — plan for multi-day trips to maximize value
Groundbait is not critical for Falcon Lake mission fish
If available, a breadcrumb-based groundbait near A Bridge of Logs can concentrate panfish for faster mission completion

boat.license_required: Advanced Oregon License ($2,000/day, $5,700/3 days, $12,600/week, 150 BC unlimited)

recommendation.features: Best stability and comfort of any kayak at this level. Paddle-powered — no motor. Lightweight and easy to handle.
recommendation.why: The Tritone Adventure is the best watercraft available at level 23 without DLC. It gives access to mid-lake fishing spots that shore anglers can't reach — perfect for targeting Smallmouth Bass near rocky structure and deep-holding trout.

dlc_option.name: Garry Scott Lyra Motor Boat
dlc_option.features: Uniq-tier echo sounder (best in game), aluminum hull, 16'4" length. Massively overpowered compared to kayaks.
dlc_option.why: If you're willing to spend real money, the Lyra is by far the best boat available at this level range. The Uniq echo sounder shows fish locations and bottom structure — huge advantage for finding Smallmouth Bass on rocky points.

fishing_tips: Boat fishing at Falcon Lake lets you reach mid-lake structure where Smallmouth Bass and Brown Trout hold
Kayak to rocky points between named spots — these unmarked areas often hold bass that shore anglers miss
Use slot 3 (jig rig) from the kayak — vertical jigging near structure is deadly for Smallmouth Bass
Deploy float rods from the kayak in deeper water — trout hold at 10-13 ft in the lake center
Anchor the kayak over structure shown on the echo sounder (Lyra only) for precise targeting
Shore spots like A Bridge of Logs are still best for panfish mission catches — save the kayak for bass and trout
boat.cost_analysis: Kayak (22,000 Cr) + Advanced License 3-day (5,700 Cr) = 27,700 Cr investment. At $200/kg Oregon Redband + $170/kg Rainbow Trout, you can recoup this in 2-3 productive days of deep-water trout fishing from the kayak.

mission.name: Falcon Exploration

Pumpkinseed.best_bait: Red Worms on #8 hook — your new slot 1 is perfectly configured for this
Pumpkinseed.best_lure: Casting Spoons, Nano Spinners — small sizes only
Pumpkinseed.best_slot: Slot 1 (Fenix + Sport Bobber + Red Worms + Kirby #8) — READY TO GO
Pumpkinseed.best_spot: A Bridge of Logs — shallow weedy areas near structure
Pumpkinseed.notes: Trophy Pumpkinseed available. Your new Sport Bobber with X Low sensitivity will detect even the lightest bites. Float fish at 3-5 ft near weed edges.

Redbreast Sunfish.best_bait: Red Worms on #8 hook — slot 1 covers this perfectly
Redbreast Sunfish.best_slot: Slot 1 (Fenix + Sport Bobber + Red Worms + Kirby #8) — READY TO GO
Redbreast Sunfish.best_spot: A Bridge of Logs — near shallow structure and log cover
Redbreast Sunfish.notes: Very small fish (max 0.6 lb). Your Kirby #8 hook and balsa Sport Bobber are ideal. Bait-only species — no lures work.

Rock Bass.best_bait: Night Crawlers or Leeches on #8 hook (wiki: Bloodworms #8)
Rock Bass.best_lure: Nano Spoons — wiki recommends Nano Spoon 4g #1
Rock Bass.best_slot: Slot 6 (ValueSpin + Nano Spinner) — swap to Nano Spoon for Rock Bass coverage
Rock Bass.best_spot: Nature's Charming Hardcore — rocky structure areas
Rock Bass.notes: Small fish (max 1.0 lb). Responds to both bait and small lures. Nano Spoons are the recommended lure type.

Smallmouth Bass.best_bait: Crawfish or Small Minnows on #1/0 hook (wiki recommended)
Smallmouth Bass.best_lure: Worm Acid Green 3" on 1/2 Oz #3/0 jighead — your new slot 3 is perfectly configured
Smallmouth Bass.best_slot: Slot 3 (JigWinner + Fluoro 12 Lb + Jighead 1/2 Oz #3/0 + Worm 3") — READY TO GO
Smallmouth Bass.best_spot: Nature's Charming Hardcore — rocky points and drop-offs
Smallmouth Bass.notes: Largest mission target (4.6 lb max). Hop the jig along rocky bottom with 2-3 second pauses — bass strike on the fall. Trophy available. Also carry Tube baits (Watermelon Seed 2") as backup.

mission_plan: 1. Start at A Bridge of Logs — deploy slot 1 (Fenix panfish rig) and slot 4 (OmniFloat Crickets) on rod stand for passive Pumpkinseed/Redbreast Sunfish catches
2. While float rods soak, actively fish slot 6 (swap to Nano Spoon) near logs for Rock Bass
3. Slot 1's Sport Bobber will show ultra-sensitive bite detection — watch for tiny dips from panfish
4. Use slots 2 and 5 to catch trout between mission bites — fill keepnet with Oregon Redband ($200/kg) for income
5. Move to Nature's Charming Hardcore for Smallmouth Bass with slot 3 (JigWinner + Worm jig) — hop jig along rocky bottom with pauses
6. Deploy slot 7 (Flies float) at Trout Lair on rod stand for passive trout income
7. Return to A Bridge of Logs to check rod stand — Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish will have accumulated
required_changes: DONE: Slot 1 — upgraded to Fenix 11'5" + Espira MLR 3500 + Fluoro .009" + Sport Bobber + Kirby #8 + Red Worms
DONE: Slot 3 — upgraded to JigWinner 8'10" + Espira MLR 3500 + Fluoro 12 Lb + Jighead 1/2 Oz #3/0 + Worm Acid Green 3"
SHOULD DO: Slot 6 — swap Nano Spinner to Nano Spoon for Rock Bass coverage
NICE TO HAVE: Bring Tube baits (Watermelon Seed 2") as backup for Smallmouth Bass
NICE TO HAVE: Bring Night Crawlers or Leeches as backup bait for Rock Bass on a float rig

missing_setups: No bait or lure currently equipped that effectively targets Rock Bass — need Night Crawlers/Leeches or swap slot 6 to Nano Spoon
xp_optimization: Complete ALL mission fish first for mission XP bonus — Pumpkinseed, Redbreast Sunfish, Rock Bass, Smallmouth Bass
Each unique species catch at Falcon Lake gives first-catch XP bonus — 8 species available
Trophy Pumpkinseed and Smallmouth Bass give 2-3x XP bonus — use proper hook sizes to increase trophy chance
Fish all 5 spots for location discovery XP — A Bridge of Logs, Nature's Charming Hardcore, The Mountain Watcher, Trout Lair, Waterfall Cascade
Use light tackle (slots 5, 6) for challenge catch XP on bigger trout
income_optimization: Oregon Redband Trout at $200/kg is the top earner at Falcon Lake — target with slots 1, 2, 4, 5, 7
Rainbow Trout ($170/kg, up to 8.2 lb) gives the highest total income per catch
Redbreast Sunfish ($170/kg) has good per-kg price but very small max size (0.6 lb) — low total per fish
Smallmouth Bass ($160/kg, up to 4.6 lb) is a solid earner and a mission target — double duty
Fill FishCastle L keepnet with trout between mission catches for maximum trip income
Falcon Lake costs $4,000 to travel + $900/day extend — you need strong income to profit on the trip
priority_changes: COMPLETED: Slot 1 upgraded to dedicated panfish rig (Fenix + Sport Bobber + Kirby #8 + Red Worms) — Pumpkinseed and Redbreast Sunfish fully covered
COMPLETED: Slot 3 upgraded to dedicated Smallmouth Bass jig rig (JigWinner + Fluoro 12 Lb + 1/2 Oz #3/0 + Worm 3") — Smallmouth Bass fully covered
MISSION CRITICAL: Add Rock Bass coverage — either swap slot 6 to Nano Spoon, or bring Night Crawlers/Leeches for a float rig
Slot 3: Switch Shad soft plastic to Worm type for better Smallmouth Bass targeting
Add fluorocarbon leaders to all float rigs — Falcon Lake water is clearer than Rocky Lake
Slot 7: Downsize hook from #1/0 to #2 or #4 for better trout bite rate
Consider buying Crawfish bait if available — it's the #1 bait for Smallmouth Bass
